isa.name: HolonNPU Program ISA
isa.ownership: Holon owns the complete program ISA; frontend implementations are replaceable microarchitectures, not ABI owners.
non_goals: No BF16, FP8, or floating-point operation classes in the current release.

operation_classes.name: SYNC
operation_classes.description: Sync, wait, and fence class required.

encoding_constants.name: SCALAR_REGISTER_COUNT
encoding_constants.description: Number of 32-bit scalar registers in the architectural frontend state; s0 is hardwired to zero.

encoding_constants.name: SCALAR_BRANCH_SCALE
encoding_constants.description: Byte scale applied to signed imm12 branch displacements relative to the current PC.

encoding_constants.name: CSR_PC
encoding_constants.description: Read-only CSR containing the PC of the executing csr.read instruction.

encoding_constants.name: CSR_INSTRET_LO
encoding_constants.description: Read-only CSR containing retired-instruction counter bits [31:0] before csr.read retires.

encoding_constants.name: CSR_INSTRET_HI
encoding_constants.description: Read-only CSR containing retired-instruction counter bits [63:32] before csr.read retires.

encoding_constants.name: CSR_PROGRAM_SIZE_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Read-only CSR containing the loaded program image size in bytes.

encoding_constants.name: CSR_LOCAL_MEM_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Read-only CSR containing the active program's local scratchpad allocation in bytes.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_VL_MINUS_ONE_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Vector length minus one in vector_config.set immediate bits [5:0].

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SEW_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Selected element-width encoding shift in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SEW_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Selected element-width encoding mask in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SIGNED
encoding_constants.description: Signed integer interpretation bit in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_ROUND_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Rounding-mode encoding shift in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_ROUND_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Rounding-mode encoding mask in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SATURATE
encoding_constants.description: Saturating arithmetic enable bit in vector_config.set immediate.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SEW_8
encoding_constants.description: Eight-bit vector element-width encoding.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SEW_16
encoding_constants.description: Sixteen-bit vector element-width encoding.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_SEW_32
encoding_constants.description: Thirty-two-bit vector element-width encoding.

encoding_constants.name: VTYPE_ROUND_RNE
encoding_constants.description: Round-to-nearest, ties-to-even mode encoding.

encoding_constants.name: VECTOR_PREDICATE_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Predicate-register selector in vector ALU immediate bits [3:0].

encoding_constants.name: VECTOR_PREDICATE_RESERVED_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Reserved vector ALU immediate bits that must be zero.

encoding_constants.name: PREDICATE_WORD_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Bytes loaded by the initial bit-packed predicate load instruction.

encoding_constants.name: DMA_WORD_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Granularity of frontend-issued DMA transfers.

encoding_constants.name: DMA_MAX_WORDS
encoding_constants.description: Maximum words transferred by one DMA instruction using the 12-bit count-minus-one field.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Size of the local per-tensor requantization command block.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_ALIGN
encoding_constants.description: Required local alignment of a requantization command block.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_MULTIPLIER_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the signed fixed-point multiplier.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_SHIFT_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the unsigned right shift in the range 0 through 31.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_ZERO_POINT_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the signed output zero point.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_CLAMP_MIN_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the signed inclusive clamp minimum.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_CLAMP_MAX_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the signed inclusive clamp maximum.

encoding_constants.name: QUANT_COMMAND_RESERVED_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the required-zero reserved command word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_BYTES
encoding_constants.description: Size and required alignment of a local matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_A_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of local A base in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_B_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of local B base in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_C_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of local C store base in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_A_STRIDE_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of A row stride in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_B_STRIDE_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of B row stride in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_C_STRIDE_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of C row stride in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_SHAPE_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of packed M/N/K/flags in a matrix command block.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_COMMAND_RESERVED_OFFSET
encoding_constants.description: Byte offset of the required-zero reserved command word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_SHAPE_M_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Active M shift in the packed matrix shape word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_SHAPE_N_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Active N shift in the packed matrix shape word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_SHAPE_K_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Active K shift in the packed matrix shape word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_SHAPE_FLAGS_SHIFT
encoding_constants.description: Command flags shift in the packed matrix shape word.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_DIMENSION_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Mask for each active matrix tile dimension.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_MAX_DIMENSION
encoding_constants.description: Maximum active M, N, or K in the first matrix implementation.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_FLAG_CLEAR
encoding_constants.description: Clear the selected accumulator before this matrix product.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_FLAG_ACCUMULATE
encoding_constants.description: Accumulate this product into an already-valid selected accumulator.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_FLAG_STORE
encoding_constants.description: Store the selected accumulator to local C after this product.

encoding_constants.name: MATRIX_FLAGS_VALID_MASK
encoding_constants.description: Valid first-generation matrix command flag bits.

instruction_classes.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL
instruction_classes.description: Integer ALU, branch, loop, address generation, and lightweight control instructions.

instruction_classes.name: PREDICATE
instruction_classes.description: Predicate register initialization and aligned bit-packed mask load.

instruction_classes.name: VECTOR_CONFIG
instruction_classes.description: Vector length, element width, signedness, rounding, and saturation configuration.

instruction_classes.name: VECTOR_ALU
instruction_classes.description: Integer elementwise add, subtract, min, max, compare, select, shift, and fixed-point helpers.

instruction_classes.name: VECTOR_MEMORY
instruction_classes.description: Contiguous scratchpad vector load and store under explicit predication.

instruction_classes.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE
instruction_classes.description: Lane shuffle, pack, unpack, and transpose helper instructions.

instruction_classes.name: VECTOR_REDUCTION
instruction_classes.description: Predicated integer sum, minimum, and maximum reductions.

instruction_classes.name: QUANTIZATION
instruction_classes.description: Requantization, clip, saturate, scale, and zero-point helpers for integer tensors.

instruction_classes.name: MATRIX
instruction_classes.description: Local-command-block issue for synchronous INT8 matrix tile operations.

instruction_classes.name: DMA
instruction_classes.description: System-memory to scratchpad and scratchpad to system-memory transfer commands.

instruction_classes.name: CSR_DEBUG
instruction_classes.description: Read-only frontend architectural state and performance-counter snapshots.

instruction_classes.name: SYNC
instruction_classes.description: DMA wait plus local-memory and DMA ordering instructions.

instruction_classes.name: SYSTEM
instruction_classes.description: Program exit and explicit-fault operations; host MMIO owns halt and debug lifecycle control.

reserved_classes.name: RESERVED_D
reserved_classes.description: Reserved for future floating-point or transformer extensions.

reserved_classes.name: RESERVED_E
reserved_classes.description: Reserved for future multi-engine and system-level extensions.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_MOVI
instructions.semantics: Write the sign-extended imm12 value to scalar register rd; writes to hardwired-zero s0 are discarded and rs1/rs2 must be zero.
instructions.description: Materialize a small signed scalar constant without consuming program-local data memory.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_ADD
instructions.semantics: Write scalar rs1 plus scalar rs2 modulo 2^32 to rd; imm12 must be zero and writes to s0 are discarded.
instructions.description: Type-independent scalar address and loop arithmetic.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_ADDI
instructions.semantics: Write scalar rs1 plus sign-extended imm12 modulo 2^32 to rd; rs2 must be zero and writes to s0 are discarded.
instructions.description: Scalar pointer increment and loop-counter update.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_LOAD
instructions.semantics: Load one aligned little-endian 32-bit word from data scratchpad address scalar rs1 plus sign-extended imm12 into rd; rs2 must be zero and writes to s0 are discarded.
instructions.description: Frontend scalar access to program arguments, loop metadata, and command construction state.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_STORE
instructions.semantics: Store scalar rs2 as one aligned little-endian 32-bit word to data scratchpad address scalar rs1 plus sign-extended imm12; rd must be zero.
instructions.description: Frontend scalar write path for kernel metadata and scalar results.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_BEQ
instructions.semantics: If scalar rs1 equals scalar rs2, set PC to current PC plus sign-extended imm12 multiplied by four; otherwise advance by one instruction. rd must be zero and the taken target must identify a complete instruction in the current program image.
instructions.description: General scalar equality branch with instruction-scaled PC-relative displacement.

instructions.name: FRONTEND_CONTROL_BNE
instructions.semantics: If scalar rs1 differs from scalar rs2, set PC to current PC plus sign-extended imm12 multiplied by four; otherwise advance by one instruction. rd must be zero and the taken target must identify a complete instruction in the current program image.
instructions.description: General scalar inequality branch used for deterministic frontend loops.

instructions.name: PREDICATE_PTRUE
instructions.semantics: Set predicate register rd active for lanes [0, VL) and inactive for all other implementation lanes.
instructions.description: VLA all-active predicate generation using current vector length.

instructions.name: PREDICATE_LOAD
instructions.semantics: Load one little-endian 32-bit bit-packed predicate word from aligned local scratchpad address imm into predicate register rd.
instructions.description: Initial scalable predicate materialization path; implementations with VL max above 32 will extend the memory format in a later ISA minor revision.

instructions.name: VECTOR_CONFIG_SET
instructions.semantics: Set VL, element width, signedness, rounding, and saturation state from one explicit immediate configuration word.
instructions.description: Vector configuration is orthogonal to operation opcodes so data-width expansion does not consume opcode space.

instructions.name: VECTOR_MEMORY_LOAD
instructions.semantics: Load active lanes selected by predicate register rs1 from VL contiguous little-endian elements using configured SEW and signedness; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Width-generic vector scratchpad load instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_MEMORY_STORE
instructions.semantics: Store active lanes selected by predicate register rs1 using configured SEW and byte strobes; inactive lanes perform no memory write.
instructions.description: Width-generic vector scratchpad store instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_ADD
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane integer add selected by immediate predicate register bits [3:0], using configured SEW and signedness with wraparound unless saturation is enabled.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector add instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_SUB
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane integer subtract using configured SEW and signedness with wraparound unless saturation is enabled.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector subtract instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_MIN
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane minimum using configured SEW and signedness.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector minimum instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_MAX
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane maximum using configured SEW and signedness.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector maximum instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_EQ
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane equality compare using configured SEW, writing canonical element value one or zero.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector equality compare instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_LT
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane less-than compare using configured SEW and signedness, writing canonical one or zero.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector less-than compare instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_SHL
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane logical left shift; shift-count width follows configured SEW.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector shift-left instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_SRL
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane logical right shift; shift-count width follows configured SEW.
instructions.description: Width-generic integer vector logical shift-right instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_SRA
instructions.semantics: Per-active-lane arithmetic right shift of the configured signed element width.
instructions.description: Width-generic signed vector arithmetic shift-right instruction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_ALU_SELECT
instructions.semantics: For every lane below VL, select vs1 when the immediate predicate bit is active and vs2 otherwise; unlike governing-predicate ALU operations, every lane below VL is written.
instructions.description: SVE-style predicate select with no implicit merge dependency on the old destination.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_GATHER
instructions.semantics: For each active lane selected by the immediate predicate, read vs1 at the unsigned lane index held in vs2; every active index must be less than VL and inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: General register-lane gather used to express shuffle, transpose, and tile-movement permutations.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_ZIP_LO
instructions.semantics: For even VL, interleave the lower VL/2 lanes of vs1 and vs2 into vd under the immediate predicate; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Lower-half lane packing primitive analogous to the first half of a scalable zip operation.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_ZIP_HI
instructions.semantics: For even VL, interleave the upper VL/2 lanes of vs1 and vs2 into vd under the immediate predicate; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Upper-half lane packing primitive paired with zip.lo.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_UNZIP_EVEN
instructions.semantics: For even VL, concatenate the even lanes of vs1 followed by the even lanes of vs2 into vd under the immediate predicate; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Lane unpacking primitive that reconstructs the first source from zip.lo and zip.hi results.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_UNZIP_ODD
instructions.semantics: For even VL, concatenate the odd lanes of vs1 followed by the odd lanes of vs2 into vd under the immediate predicate; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Lane unpacking primitive that reconstructs the second source from zip.lo and zip.hi results.

instructions.name: VECTOR_PERMUTE_TRANSPOSE4
instructions.semantics: With VL exactly 16 and rs2 equal to zero, treat vs1 as one row-major 4x4 tile and write its transpose to vd under the immediate predicate; inactive destination lanes are preserved.
instructions.description: Explicit in-register 4x4 transpose helper for tensor tile movement.

instructions.name: VECTOR_REDUCTION_SUM
instructions.semantics: Reduce active source lanes selected by the immediate predicate with configured-width wraparound addition into destination lane zero; an empty active set produces zero.
instructions.description: Predicate-aware integer sum reduction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_REDUCTION_MIN
instructions.semantics: Reduce active source lanes using configured signedness into destination lane zero; an empty active set produces the greatest representable configured element.
instructions.description: Predicate-aware integer minimum reduction.

instructions.name: VECTOR_REDUCTION_MAX
instructions.semantics: Reduce active source lanes using configured signedness into destination lane zero; an empty active set produces the least representable configured element.
instructions.description: Predicate-aware integer maximum reduction.

instructions.name: QUANTIZATION_REQUANTIZE
instructions.semantics: For active lanes selected by predicate rs2, multiply signed or unsigned source rs1 by the command multiplier, right shift with round-to-nearest ties-to-even, add zero point, clamp inclusively, canonicalize to configured SEW/signedness, and preserve inactive destination lanes. The aligned local command block at imm contains multiplier, shift, zero point, clamp minimum, clamp maximum, and a required-zero word.
instructions.description: Per-tensor fixed-point requantization, clipping, saturation, and narrowing helper.

instructions.name: MATRIX_GEMM
instructions.semantics: Issue one active INT8 MxK by KxN tile product described by the aligned local command block at imm to accumulator rd; rs1 and rs2 must be zero.
instructions.description: Frontend-issued B-weight-stationary INT8 matrix tile micro-op.

instructions.name: CSR_DEBUG_READ
instructions.semantics: Read the read-only frontend CSR selected by imm12 into scalar register rd; rs1 and rs2 must be zero, writes to s0 are discarded, and an unknown CSR selector faults precisely without retiring.
instructions.description: Architectural frontend state and retired-instruction snapshot read.

instructions.name: SYSTEM_EXIT
instructions.semantics: Terminate the active Holon program with DONE.
instructions.description: Program terminal success instruction.

instructions.name: SYSTEM_FAULT
instructions.semantics: Terminate with EXPLICIT_PROGRAM_FAULT when imm12 is zero; any nonzero reserved immediate produces ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION without retiring.
instructions.description: Program terminal explicit fault instruction.

instructions.name: DMA_LOAD
instructions.semantics: Copy imm12+1 aligned 32-bit words from the 64-bit system address in {scalar[rs1], scalar[rd]} into local scratchpad at the 32-bit byte address in scalar[rs2].
instructions.description: Register-addressed frontend-issued DMA system-to-scratchpad load instruction.

instructions.name: DMA_STORE
instructions.semantics: Copy imm12+1 aligned 32-bit words from local scratchpad at the 32-bit byte address in scalar[rs2] into the 64-bit system address in {scalar[rs1], scalar[rd]}.
instructions.description: Register-addressed frontend-issued DMA scratchpad-to-system store instruction.

instructions.name: SYNC_WAIT_DMA
instructions.semantics: Wait until all prior frontend-issued DMA commands have completed and their effects are architecturally visible.
instructions.description: DMA completion ordering point. In the first in-order DMA implementation, DMA instructions already retire after completion, so this instruction retires as an explicit no-op order point.

instructions.name: SYNC_FENCE_LOCAL
instructions.semantics: Order prior local memory writes before later local memory reads or engine issue.
instructions.description: Local scratchpad ordering point for future multi-client local-memory arbitration.

instructions.name: SYNC_FENCE_DMA
instructions.semantics: Order prior local memory writes before later DMA stores and order prior DMA loads before later consumers.
instructions.description: DMA/local-memory ordering point for frontend-visible transfer ordering.
